body.title: Add Updated <timestamp> markers to architectural doc blocks for drift detection
body.result: Updated 2026-04-29T17:08:53Z added to 9 HIGH-inertia doc blocks: brainstem/core_patterns.py, memory/models.py, cognition/reasoners/base.py, memory/cortex.py, db_proxy.py, pending_replies.py, word_graph.py, pe_chain.py, main.py
body.description:
During high churn, architectural docs at the top of code files drift from the code silently. Fix: each file's doc block ends with a comment line 'Updated YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ'. Any reader (Haiku builder, audit step) can compare that timestamp against the file's last commit time to flag stale docs without reading the whole file.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/brainstem/ (all modules), wild_igor/igor/memory/models.py, wild_igor/igor/cognition/reasoners/base.py, and other load-bearing subsystems with doc blocks. Step 1 = HIGH-inertia files; Step 2 = remaining.
**Design rules:** docs-in-code applies — doc blocks are canonical; the timestamp makes their freshness mechanically verifiable.
**Scope boundary:** IN: Add/update 'Updated YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' as the last comment line of each doc block in load-bearing files. OUT: No Haiku builder wiring in this ticket; no automated staleness check (separate ticket).
**Test plan:** No automated tests. Manual: grep each file's doc block, verify Updated line exists and timestamp is within 7 days of last commit (spot-check 5 files).
